time_budget.note: Operator contains 8x ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn (FP32 SIMT, tensor_core_active_pct=0.0) and 2x fmha_cutlassF (tensor_core_active_pct~39%). The aggregated tensor_core_active_pct=11.3 is diluted by the dominant GEMM kernel mass. The tensor_core_idle classification is driven by the embedded GEMM kernels. All ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn invocations are on the FP32 SIMT dispatch path.
time_budget.fix: Cast all linear/mm inputs to BF16 or FP16. This routes cuBLAS dispatch from ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn (FP32 SIMT) to sm80_xmma_gemm_f16f16 or sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16 (Tensor Core HMMA). Expected 4-8x throughput improvement on the GEMM kernels. SDPA FP32->FP16 cast also enables the FlashAttention FP16 path with lower register pressure.

secondary_flags.register_pressure: fmha kernels use 168 registers/thread (> 128 limit) causing 9,428,992 bytes of local memory spills. Upgrading to FlashAttention-2 (xformers memory_efficient_attention or F.scaled_dot_product_attention with flash_sdp) reduces register usage and eliminates spills.

time_budget.note: 4 iterations of FP32 SIMT GEMM (M=4096, N=512, K=512). tensor_core_active_pct=0.0 exactly. SM throughput is 84.5% — the compute pipeline is saturated on SIMT, not Tensor Cores. Casting to BF16/FP16 would shift all compute to the Tensor Core pipeline.
time_budget.fix: Cast inputs to BF16/FP16 before aten::mm (model-level dtype change: model.half() or model.bfloat16()). Routes dispatch to sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_tn Tensor Core path. SM throughput should remain near 84% but now leverages Tensor Core throughput (312 BF16 TFLOPS vs 19.5 FP32 TFLOPS on A100).

time_budget.note: achieved_occupancy=15.85% is below the 20% threshold. Grid=(8,8,8) = 512 CTAs over 108 SMs = 5 theoretical waves, but actual occupancy is reduced by the 168 registers/thread which limits warps/SM to roughly 16% of peak. The kernel also spills 4,714,496 bytes to local memory (registers to L2/HBM), which directly explains the low SM utilization (45.5%) and the residual memory latency stalls.
time_budget.fix: Replace xformers fmha_cutlassF_f32 with torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention (PyTorch 2.0+) with FP16/BF16 input. This dispatches to the FlashAttention-2 Triton kernel (fewer registers, no local spills) and eliminates the register pressure root cause. The 168 register/thread limit is architecture-imposed for this specific CUTLASS kernel variant.

secondary_flags.register_pressure: CRITICAL — 168 registers/thread exceeds the 128 limit; local_memory_spills=4,714,496 bytes. Spills go to L2/HBM, adding memory traffic that explains the gap between sm_throughput_pct=45.5% and tensor_core_active_pct=39.6%.

time_budget.note: Identical kernel and metrics to aten::_efficient_attention_forward_0. Second SDPA invocation. Same root cause: FP32 CUTLASS FlashAttention with 168 registers/thread causing local memory spills.
time_budget.fix: Same fix as aten::_efficient_attention_forward_0: use F.scaled_dot_product_attention with BF16/FP16 dtype.

time_budget.note: Third SDPA invocation. Identical root cause to the two above.

time_budget.fix: BF16/FP16 dtype cast. Same as aten::mm_0.

time_budget.note: Below 1% wall-time threshold. Memory throughput 49.6% indicates mild memory-bound behavior but this operator is negligible in the current profile.

edge_case_details.ncu_replay_timing: ALL duration_ns values are from ncu hardware-counter replay passes, not real execution wall time. ncu replay is typically 2-5x longer than actual runtime due to serialization and counter collection overhead. Use these values for relative comparison between operators only, not for absolute latency estimates.
edge_case_details.fused_kernel_double_count: Four operators are marked is_fused=true: layer::unique::prologue_0, aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=14, aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=36, aten::_unsafe_view_0. Their duration is logically shared with parent operators. The total_attributed_ns of 4,721,771 ns should not be interpreted as sequential wall time — the fused operators' kernels are included within the prologue's 16-kernel span as well.

architecture_notes: NVIDIA A100-SXM4-80GB (Ampere): 108 SMs, peak BF16 = 312 TFLOPS, peak FP32 = 19.5 TFLOPS, HBM bandwidth = 2.0 TB/s, ridge point BF16 = 156 FLOP/byte. warp_cycles_per_instruction counter is available on Ampere but was not collected in this profile run (all values null); eligible_cycles_pct is used as the latency-bound proxy. All GEMM kernels show eligible_cycles_pct ~58%, indicating schedulers are kept busy — latency hiding is adequate within the active SMs, confirming the bottleneck is compute (SIMT path) not latency.

bottleneck_summary.root_cause: All GEMM/mm kernels (ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn) are executing on the FP32 SIMT path with tensor_core_active_pct=0.0. The model is running in FP32 precision. On A100, this wastes 16x the available compute: A100 delivers 312 BF16 TFLOPS via Tensor Cores vs 19.5 FP32 TFLOPS via SIMT. The SDPA attention kernels (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80) are on the FP32 CUTLASS path with 168 registers/thread causing local memory spills, reducing occupancy to ~16% and introducing secondary register-pressure stalls.
bottleneck_summary.systemic_pattern: 100% of matrix multiplication time (all 12 aten::mm operators) uses the FP32 SIMT kernel. This is a single-fix root cause: the model was not cast to a reduced precision dtype before compilation.

optimization_headroom.description: Casting the model to BF16 or FP16 addresses the primary bottleneck across all mm/linear operators (82.7% of attributed time). On A100, BF16 Tensor Core throughput is 16x that of FP32 SIMT (312 vs 19.5 TFLOPS). The GEMM kernels currently run at SM throughput ~84% of SIMT peak — after the dtype cast the same SM utilization will deliver 16x more useful FLOPS per cycle, making the GEMMs bandwidth-limited rather than compute-limited. The SDPA kernels will also benefit: switching to F.scaled_dot_product_attention with FP16 eliminates the CUTLASS FP32 register spill path and is expected to reduce SDPA time by 30-50% through lower register pressure and higher occupancy.
optimization_headroom.estimated_speedup_gemm_kernels: up to 8-12x per kernel (real-world speedup is lower than theoretical 16x due to memory bandwidth becoming the new bottleneck after TC activation)
optimization_headroom.single_change_required: model = model.to(torch.bfloat16) before torch.compile(), or torch.set_default_dtype(torch.bfloat16) at model construction
